title: Strait of Hormuz transits suspended; GULF HORIZON unable to reach Jebel Ali
impact: Carriers refusing Strait of Hormuz transits after the 2026 Gulf crisis. Jebel-Ali-bound vessels are diverting or sheltering; goods may be discharged at Khor Fakkan outside the Gulf instead of the contracted port of discharge (Jebel Ali). ETA blown; CIF delivery to named port at risk.
real_basis: Strait of Hormuz Crisis 2026: ~138 container ships (~470k TEU) trapped in the Persian Gulf per Alphaliner; carriers will not resume Gulf transits until safe; Iran charging USD 1M+ per vessel; elevated through at least Q3 2026.

title: Jebel Ali / Gulf hub congestion as vessels shelter in port
impact: Vessels sheltering at Jebel Ali, Abu Dhabi and Doha; berth and discharge delays expected at the port of discharge.
real_basis: Diverted and sheltering vessels competing for berth space at Gulf hubs; transshipment congestion at Colombo, Singapore and Nhava Sheva.

title: War-risk surcharge and Hormuz transit toll raise Gulf shipping cost
impact: Marine war-risk premiums for Gulf transit have spiked and carriers add war-risk surcharges; Iran charging USD 1M+ per vessel. Under CIF the seller pays freight and insurance to the port of discharge, so these costs and any coverage gap fall on the seller.
real_basis: 2026-02-28 US/Israel strikes on Iran (Operation Epic Fury); ceasefire 2026-04-07 but Gulf transits not resumed; war-risk premiums elevated through Q3 2026.

title: Houthi drone and missile attacks force Red Sea convoy diversion around Cape of Good Hope
impact: Carriers are diverting Asia-Europe services away from the Red Sea and Bab el-Mandeb around the Cape of Good Hope, adding 10-14 days and war-risk surcharges. Under CIF the seller pays freight and insurance to the port of discharge, so the diversion cost and any war-risk coverage gap fall on the seller.
real_basis: Since late 2023 Houthi attacks in the Red Sea / Bab el-Mandeb have pushed most container carriers onto the longer Cape route; Suez transits remain far below normal into 2026.

title: Typhoon forces Shanghai port closure and berth congestion at Yangshan
impact: A typhoon system in the East China Sea has closed Shanghai and Ningbo terminals and suspended pilotage; sailings from the port of loading are delayed, threatening the latest shipment date. Under CIF a delay before loading is the seller's risk and can breach the LC latest shipment date.
real_basis: East China Sea typhoon season peaks Jul-Sep; Shanghai/Ningbo routinely suspend operations and pilotage for 24-72h during landfall windows.
